framework.jurisdiction: United States; used internationally in SOC 2 engagements
framework.name: 2017 Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy (With Revised Points of Focus – 2022)
framework.scope: The Trust Services Criteria are AICPA control criteria used in attestation or consulting engagements to evaluate and report on controls over the security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, or privacy of information and systems used to provide products or services. They are designed for service organizations and can be applied across an entire entity or to a subsidiary, division, or operating unit, including systems used to process users’ data.[AICPA](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022)
framework.applies_to: Service organizations of any size and industry that provide products or services using systems subject to SOC 2 reporting; commonly cloud, SaaS, data processing, managed services, fintech, healthcare vendors, and other outsourced service providers. Can cover one or more trust services categories (security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, privacy) and may be scoped to an entity, subsidiary, division, or operating unit.
framework.penalties: No direct statutory penalty regime; compliance is voluntary and enforcement is contractual or market-based through customer requirements, procurement, and independent CPA attestation. No maximum penalty is specified by the AICPA criteria themselves.

control.statement: Management demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values and establishes oversight of the entity’s internal control responsibilities.
The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.
The entity identifies and assesses risks to the achievement of objectives across the system and selects suitable responses.
The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to acceptable levels.
The entity selects and develops general controls over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
The entity implements activities to obtain and use relevant quality information to support internal control.
The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies to those responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board as appropriate.
The entity maintains, monitors, and updates its infrastructure, software, and data to meet its availability commitments and recover from disruptions.
The entity performs processing activities completely, accurately, timely, and authorized as required by the processing integrity objective.
The entity identifies and addresses privacy commitments, legal requirements, and disclosures for personal information throughout its lifecycle.
